addedBlocks:
## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- rbk-wrangle: Muster, then roster RBK and JJK heats as two space-padded plain-text blocks
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Docket:
  ## Character
  Intricate but mechanical refactor; the one judgment is the establish/re-key boundary.
  
  Close the credential-lifecycle hole on director + retriever. The fused invest does
  SA-create + Binding-grant + Key-create, and its only inverse (divest) does SA-delete
  alone — so Bindings are never revoked (H1) and re-key churns the whole SA (H2).
  
  Split the fused invest into three operations:
  - establish (idempotent — ensure SA exists + grant its Bindings once)
  - re-key (Key-only — delete all Keys, add one; the blunt-kill guarantee preserved)
  - teardown (revoke Bindings, then delete SA — the never-wired inverse; only at genuine retirement)
  
  Source: `Memos/memo-20260604-credential-churn-leak-and-propagation-races.md` — objects,
  the three lifecycles, H1/H2, and the 8 scope notes (idempotent establish; re-key presumes
  established; keep the DRS-400 in rbgi's retry tolerance at establish; governor out of scope).
  
  Done: re-key touches only Keys; Bindings granted once and revoked only on teardown;
  standing-depot reruns stop accruing `deleted:` tombstones.

# jjezs_paddock ₣BY

# Paddock: rbk-08-credential-repairs

## Context

(Describe the initiative's background and goals)

## References

(List relevant files, docs, or prior work)


# jjezs_pace ₢BYAAA

## Character
Intricate but mechanical refactor; the one judgment is the establish/re-key boundary.

Close the credential-lifecycle hole on director + retriever. The fused invest does
SA-create + Binding-grant + Key-create, and its only inverse (divest) does SA-delete
alone — so Bindings are never revoked (H1) and re-key churns the whole SA (H2).

Split the fused invest into three operations:
- establish (idempotent — ensure SA exists + grant its Bindings once)
- re-key (Key-only — delete all Keys, add one; the blunt-kill guarantee preserved)
- teardown (revoke Bindings, then delete SA — the never-wired inverse; only at genuine retirement)

Source: `Memos/memo-20260604-credential-churn-leak-and-propagation-races.md` — objects,
the three lifecycles, H1/H2, and the 8 scope notes (idempotent establish; re-key presumes
established; keep the DRS-400 in rbgi's retry tolerance at establish; governor out of scope).

Done: re-key touches only Keys; Bindings granted once and revoked only on teardown;
standing-depot reruns stop accruing `deleted:` tombstones.

content.content:
1	# Credential lifecycle: the Binding-revoke hole (keyfile tier)
2	
3	*2026-06-04. Surfaced in heat ₣BV while bringing the `blockade` (moriah / airgap)
4	crucible up on macOS Docker Desktop (branch `bv-macos-crucible-20260603-BVAAE`);
5	repair tracked under `rbk-08-credential-repairs`. Depot under study: the
6	canonical standing depot `cancbhm-d-canest3bhm100001`, org `247899326218`.*
7	
8	## Headline
9	
10	The keyfile credential tier (RBSHR's name for the current model: each role holds
11	an RBRA key file, possession = identity) **fuses three independent object
12	lifecycles into one operation**, and never wires the revoke half of one of them.
13	The result: IAM bindings accumulate without bound, and re-credentialing trips two
14	separate Google eventual-consistency edges. All of it follows from one structural
15	hole, stated precisely below. None of it is macOS-specific.
16	
17	## Objects
18	
19	- **SA** — a GCP service account, e.g. `director-canest-dir@…`. Identity is
20	  email **+ uid**; recreating the same name yields a *new* uid.
21	- **Key** — credential material *on* an SA. Serialized to disk as the **RBRA**
22	  file the operator holds. (Key = GCP-side; RBRA = its delivery.)
23	- **Binding** — one `(role → member)` entry; the member *is* an SA; it lives on
24	  a **Resource**.
25	- **Resource** — a thing carrying an IAM policy: the **project**, the **GAR
26	  repo**, the **Mason SA**.
27	
28	## Three independent lifecycles
29	
30	1. **SA**: create → exists → delete. *On delete, GCP cannot drop bindings whose
31	   member vanished — it retags them `deleted:…?uid=` and keeps them.*
32	2. **Key**: add → valid → delete. *Deleting a Key is a blunt kill of one
33	   credential; the SA is untouched.*
34	3. **Binding**: grant (add member) → active → **revoke (remove member)**. Keyed
35	   by member = SA.
36	
37	Re-credentialing an operator *requires only the Key lifecycle*: add a new Key,
38	kill the old one.
39	
40	## The hole
41	
42	The code fuses "create the identity" with "issue a credential." One invest does
43	**SA-create + Binding-grant + Key-create** together; its only inverse (divest)
44	does **SA-delete** alone. From that fusion, two defects:
45	
46	- **H1 — the Binding lifecycle is half-wired.** `grant` runs at invest;
47	  **`revoke` is never called anywhere.** Divest deletes the SA and stops
48	  (`zrbgg_divest_role` never opens an IAM policy). So Bindings are `+1` per
49	  invest and `0` per divest — monotonic, unbounded.
50	- **H2 — re-key invokes the wrong lifecycle.** "Re-key" runs the **SA**
51	  lifecycle (delete + recreate — chosen as a blunt guarantee the old Key is
52	  dead) when it only needed the **Key** lifecycle. So the SA *and its Bindings*
53	  churn every issuance, when only the Key had to.
54	
55	## The three faces (all consequences of the hole)
56	
57	- **Binding leak ← H1.** Bindings granted, never revoked, accumulate as
58	  `deleted:` ghosts on every Resource. Observed: **project 145** (69 director,
59	  46 retriever, 30 governor-owner), **GAR repo 23**, **Mason SA 23**. *Benign so
60	  far — Experiment A (below) shows the 145-ghost policy still resubmits cleanly —
61	  but it marches toward GCP's ~1500-member allow-policy cap independent of any
62	  org policy.* A comment in `rbgi_add_project_iam_role` already records the
63	  ghosts as a known, "accepted" cost; they have now become unbounded.
64	- **create→grant race ← H2.** Each cycle mints a *fresh* SA and immediately
65	  grants it. The org enforces Domain Restricted Sharing
66	  (`iam.allowedPolicyMemberDomains`, allow-listing customer `C02ystilf` =
67	  scaleinvariant.org); a brand-new SA is not yet recognized as in-customer, so
68	  the grant is rejected (HTTP 400, "incompatible with the provided policy"), and
69	  `rbgi`'s retry tolerance does **not** cover that error — so it dies on the
70	  first try. *This blocked `blockade`.* Google documents the propagation lag
71	  (≈2 min, up to 7+). [restricting-domains], [troubleshoot-org-policies]
72	- **delete→recreate race ← H2.** Divest deletes the SA and reports it gone, but
73	  the immediate re-create's preflight still sees it ("already exists"). *This
74	  blocked `dogfight`* the next morning. A state check minutes later showed the SA
75	  fully gone with no soft-delete reservation — the create-preflight simply read
76	  a not-yet-converged view.
77	
78	Both races are **intermittent and host-independent** (server-side); they pass
79	routinely on cerebro/Cygwin and lost on macOS two days running. The macOS
80	crucible itself is sound — `siege` is 60/60 green there.
81	
82	## The fix — separate the lifecycles
83	
84	Stop fusing. Split the one invest into two operations:
85	
86	- **Establish** (one-time per SA — at depot setup / operator enrollment):
87	  ensure the SA exists and **grant its Bindings once**. Idempotent — a no-op when
88	  already established.
89	- **Re-key** (every issuance): **rotate the Key only** — delete all existing
90	  Keys (the blunt kill, preserved), add one. Never touches the SA or its
91	  Bindings.
92	- **Teardown** (rare — genuine retirement): the missing inverse — **revoke
93	  Bindings, then delete the SA** (H1's never-wired half), run once at end-of-life
94	  rather than every cycle.
95	
96	This closes H1 (Bindings granted once, revoked on real teardown — never churned)
97	and H2 (re-key is Key-only; the SA never gets recreated). Scope notes:
98	
99	1. It's a refactor, not a line: one fused verb → establish / re-key (+ a real
100	   teardown).
101	2. Establish must be **idempotent** ("ensure," not "create").
102	3. Re-key **presumes established** (assert SA + Bindings present, else establish).
103	4. Blunt-kill preserved: re-key = delete *all* Keys, add one.
104	5. The create→grant DRS race doesn't vanish — it **relocates to establish**
105	   (now ≈once per identity, off the hot path). Keep the DRS-400 in `rbgi`'s
106	   retry tolerance there as cheap insurance.
107	6. Existing ghosts (145 + 23 + 23) need a **one-time sweep** — the split stops
108	   new ones, doesn't clean old. Safe: only `deleted:*` removed (Experiment A
109	   shows the policy is writable).
110	7. Cross-host see-saw is **orthogonal** — a shared identity re-keyed bluntly
111	   invalidates other holders either way; unchanged.
112	8. **Governor out of scope** (deliberate single-governor, datestamped,
113	   low-churn simplification). Fix targets director/retriever.
114	
115	## Footnote: federation
116	
117	The roadmap's operator-federation tier (RBSHR; `memo-20260527-operator-credential-models.md`)
118	grants capabilities to stable `principal://` subjects with central revocation —
119	no per-cycle SA at all, so it would obviate this hole entirely. It is the
120	**eventual successor tier, not the MVP fix**; the keyfile tier stays and is
121	repaired in place as above.
122	
123	## Evidence appendix
124	
125	- DRS + policy state: `gcloud resource-manager org-policies …`,
126	  `gcloud projects get-iam-policy cancbhm-d-canest3bhm100001` (payor-token, 06-04).
127	  DRS allow-list = `C02ystilf`.
128	- **Experiment A** — `set-iam-policy` of the unchanged 145-ghost project policy
129	  → HTTP 200. Ghosts do not block grants.
130	- **Experiment B′** — grant `roles/cloudbuild.builds.editor` to the now-propagated
131	  director SA → HTTP 200. Freshness was the only variable.
132	- create→grant race: `logs-buk/hist-rbw-adI-sh-20260603-103842-26773-301.txt`.
133	- delete→recreate race: `logs-buk/hist-rbw-adI-sh-20260604-092730-7113-822.txt`,
134	  suite `logs-buk/hist-rbw-ts-dogfight-20260604-092601-3729-496.txt`.
135	- Code: `rbgi_add_project_iam_role` + the accepted-ghost comment
136	  (`Tools/rbk/rbgi_IAM.sh`); `zrbgg_divest_role`, `rbgg_invest_director`,
137	  `rbgg_invest_retriever` (`Tools/rbk/rbgg_Governor.sh`);
138	  `rbtdrk_role_invest_impl` (`Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrk_canonical.rs`).
139	
140	[restricting-domains]: https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/docs/organization-policy/restricting-domains
141	[troubleshoot-org-policies]: https://docs.cloud.google.com/iam/docs/troubleshoot-org-policies
142

# Credential lifecycle: the Binding-revoke hole (keyfile tier)

*2026-06-04. Surfaced in heat ₣BV while bringing the `blockade` (moriah / airgap)
crucible up on macOS Docker Desktop (branch `bv-macos-crucible-20260603-BVAAE`);
repair tracked under `rbk-08-credential-repairs`. Depot under study: the
canonical standing depot `cancbhm-d-canest3bhm100001`, org `247899326218`.*

## Headline

The keyfile credential tier (RBSHR's name for the current model: each role holds
an RBRA key file, possession = identity) **fuses three independent object
lifecycles into one operation**, and never wires the revoke half of one of them.
The result: IAM bindings accumulate without bound, and re-credentialing trips two
separate Google eventual-consistency edges. All of it follows from one structural
hole, stated precisely below. None of it is macOS-specific.

## Objects

- **SA** — a GCP service account, e.g. `director-canest-dir@…`. Identity is
  email **+ uid**; recreating the same name yields a *new* uid.
- **Key** — credential material *on* an SA. Serialized to disk as the **RBRA**
  file the operator holds. (Key = GCP-side; RBRA = its delivery.)
- **Binding** — one `(role → member)` entry; the member *is* an SA; it lives on
  a **Resource**.
- **Resource** — a thing carrying an IAM policy: the **project**, the **GAR
  repo**, the **Mason SA**.

## Three independent lifecycles

1. **SA**: create → exists → delete. *On delete, GCP cannot drop bindings whose
   member vanished — it retags them `deleted:…?uid=` and keeps them.*
2. **Key**: add → valid → delete. *Deleting a Key is a blunt kill of one
   credential; the SA is untouched.*
3. **Binding**: grant (add member) → active → **revoke (remove member)**. Keyed
   by member = SA.

Re-credentialing an operator *requires only the Key lifecycle*: add a new Key,
kill the old one.

## The hole

The code fuses "create the identity" with "issue a credential." One invest does
**SA-create + Binding-grant + Key-create** together; its only inverse (divest)
does **SA-delete** alone. From that fusion, two defects:

- **H1 — the Binding lifecycle is half-wired.** `grant` runs at invest;
  **`revoke` is never called anywhere.** Divest deletes the SA and stops
  (`zrbgg_divest_role` never opens an IAM policy). So Bindings are `+1` per
  invest and `0` per divest — monotonic, unbounded.
- **H2 — re-key invokes the wrong lifecycle.** "Re-key" runs the **SA**
  lifecycle (delete + recreate — chosen as a blunt guarantee the old Key is
  dead) when it only needed the **Key** lifecycle. So the SA *and its Bindings*
  churn every issuance, when only the Key had to.

## The three faces (all consequences of the hole)

- **Binding leak ← H1.** Bindings granted, never revoked, accumulate as
  `deleted:` ghosts on every Resource. Observed: **project 145** (69 director,
  46 retriever, 30 governor-owner), **GAR repo 23**, **Mason SA 23**. *Benign so
  far — Experiment A (below) shows the 145-ghost policy still resubmits cleanly —
  but it marches toward GCP's ~1500-member allow-policy cap independent of any
  org policy.* A comment in `rbgi_add_project_iam_role` already records the
  ghosts as a known, "accepted" cost; they have now become unbounded.
- **create→grant race ← H2.** Each cycle mints a *fresh* SA and immediately
  grants it. The org enforces Domain Restricted Sharing
  (`iam.allowedPolicyMemberDomains`, allow-listing customer `C02ystilf` =
  scaleinvariant.org); a brand-new SA is not yet recognized as in-customer, so
  the grant is rejected (HTTP 400, "incompatible with the provided policy"), and
  `rbgi`'s retry tolerance does **not** cover that error — so it dies on the
  first try. *This blocked `blockade`.* Google documents the propagation lag
  (≈2 min, up to 7+). [restricting-domains], [troubleshoot-org-policies]
- **delete→recreate race ← H2.** Divest deletes the SA and reports it gone, but
  the immediate re-create's preflight still sees it ("already exists"). *This
  blocked `dogfight`* the next morning. A state check minutes later showed the SA
  fully gone with no soft-delete reservation — the create-preflight simply read
  a not-yet-converged view.

Both races are **intermittent and host-independent** (server-side); they pass
routinely on cerebro/Cygwin and lost on macOS two days running. The macOS
crucible itself is sound — `siege` is 60/60 green there.

## The fix — separate the lifecycles

Stop fusing. Split the one invest into two operations:

- **Establish** (one-time per SA — at depot setup / operator enrollment):
  ensure the SA exists and **grant its Bindings once**. Idempotent — a no-op when
  already established.
- **Re-key** (every issuance): **rotate the Key only** — delete all existing
  Keys (the blunt kill, preserved), add one. Never touches the SA or its
  Bindings.
- **Teardown** (rare — genuine retirement): the missing inverse — **revoke
  Bindings, then delete the SA** (H1's never-wired half), run once at end-of-life
  rather than every cycle.

This closes H1 (Bindings granted once, revoked on real teardown — never churned)
and H2 (re-key is Key-only; the SA never gets recreated). Scope notes:

1. It's a refactor, not a line: one fused verb → establish / re-key (+ a real
   teardown).
2. Establish must be **idempotent** ("ensure," not "create").
3. Re-key **presumes established** (assert SA + Bindings present, else establish).
4. Blunt-kill preserved: re-key = delete *all* Keys, add one.
5. The create→grant DRS race doesn't vanish — it **relocates to establish**
   (now ≈once per identity, off the hot path). Keep the DRS-400 in `rbgi`'s
   retry tolerance there as cheap insurance.
6. Existing ghosts (145 + 23 + 23) need a **one-time sweep** — the split stops
   new ones, doesn't clean old. Safe: only `deleted:*` removed (Experiment A
   shows the policy is writable).
7. Cross-host see-saw is **orthogonal** — a shared identity re-keyed bluntly
   invalidates other holders either way; unchanged.
8. **Governor out of scope** (deliberate single-governor, datestamped,
   low-churn simplification). Fix targets director/retriever.

## Footnote: federation

The roadmap's operator-federation tier (RBSHR; `memo-20260527-operator-credential-models.md`)
grants capabilities to stable `principal://` subjects with central revocation —
no per-cycle SA at all, so it would obviate this hole entirely. It is the
**eventual successor tier, not the MVP fix**; the keyfile tier stays and is
repaired in place as above.

## Evidence appendix

- DRS + policy state: `gcloud resource-manager org-policies …`,
  `gcloud projects get-iam-policy cancbhm-d-canest3bhm100001` (payor-token, 06-04).
  DRS allow-list = `C02ystilf`.
- **Experiment A** — `set-iam-policy` of the unchanged 145-ghost project policy
  → HTTP 200. Ghosts do not block grants.
- **Experiment B′** — grant `roles/cloudbuild.builds.editor` to the now-propagated
  director SA → HTTP 200. Freshness was the only variable.
- create→grant race: `logs-buk/hist-rbw-adI-sh-20260603-103842-26773-301.txt`.
- delete→recreate race: `logs-buk/hist-rbw-adI-sh-20260604-092730-7113-822.txt`,
  suite `logs-buk/hist-rbw-ts-dogfight-20260604-092601-3729-496.txt`.
- Code: `rbgi_add_project_iam_role` + the accepted-ghost comment
  (`Tools/rbk/rbgi_IAM.sh`); `zrbgg_divest_role`, `rbgg_invest_director`,
  `rbgg_invest_retriever` (`Tools/rbk/rbgg_Governor.sh`);
  `rbtdrk_role_invest_impl` (`Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrk_canonical.rs`).

[restricting-domains]: https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/docs/organization-policy/restricting-domains
[troubleshoot-org-policies]: https://docs.cloud.google.com/iam/docs/troubleshoot-org-policies

content.text: The memo gives me the full shape. Let me read the second pace's docket to see the whole heat.

## Character
Design exploration — judgment, not mechanical. Output is a recommendation, not an implementation.

Evaluate refactoring RBRA from one-file-per-role-type (director, retriever — each with its
own cult verbs) to one-file-per-person carrying any combination of roles. Collapses the
role-typed verb surface and aligns the keyfile tier with the federation model's
principal -> capability-set shape (smoother eventual migration).

The lifecycle split is its enabler: once identity (establish) and credential (re-key) are
separated, "a principal with a role-set" is the natural next shape.

Key question to resolve: does per-person multi-role preserve least-privilege? Today's
role-typed SAs enforce it by construction — a retriever cannot act as director. A per-person
SA is only as least-privilege as the grants chosen; show the security guarantee survives,
not just the convenience.

Source: the federation footnote in `Memos/memo-20260604-credential-churn-leak-and-propagation-races.md`;
`Memos/memo-20260527-operator-credential-models.md`.

Done: a decision/recommendation (do / don't / how), not implementation.

I'm mounted on **₣BY (rbk-08-credential-repairs)**. Here's where I'm starting from.

## ₢BYAAA — lifecycle-split-establish-rekey *(next, [rough])*

**Goal in one sentence:** Stop the fused `invest` from doing SA-create + Binding-grant + Key-create as one verb — split it into three independent operations (**establish** / **re-key** / **teardown**) so that re-credentialing only rotates Keys, Bindings are granted once and revoked only at real retirement, and the IAM-binding leak stops growing.

**Heat context:** `rbk-08-credential-repairs`, 0/2 done. Just one prior commit — the grounding memo. The paddock is a bare template (Context/References still placeholders), so the entire shape lives in the docket + the source memo, which I've read in full. The second pace (₢BYAAB) is a *design recommendation* on collapsing RBRA to one-file-per-person — explicitly downstream of this split, not blocked work.

**The structural hole (from the memo):** one `invest` fuses three lifecycles whose only inverse (`divest`) deletes the SA alone. Two defects fall out:
- **H1** — `revoke` is never wired anywhere; `zrbgg_divest_role` deletes the SA without touching IAM policy, so `deleted:` ghost bindings accumulate unbounded (observed: 145 on the project, 23 on GAR repo, 23 on Mason SA).
- **H2** — "re-key" runs the *SA* lifecycle (delete + recreate) when it only needed the *Key* lifecycle, churning the SA + its Bindings every issuance and tripping two Google eventual-consistency races (create→grant DRS-400, delete→recreate "already exists").

**Name assessment:** silks `lifecycle-split-establish-rekey` fits the thrust but drops the third operation — **teardown** (the never-wired revoke half) is load-bearing to closing H1. Minor; I'd leave it unless you want `...establish-rekey-teardown`.

## Proposed approach (4 bullets)

- **Mint + design the new verb surface** — one fused `invest` → `establish` (idempotent: ensure SA + grant Bindings once) / `re-key` (Key-only: delete all Keys, add one — blunt-kill preserved) / `teardown` (revoke Bindings → delete SA). New `rbw-a*` colophons need minting; that's the one design seam inside the "mechanical" work.
- **Wire the missing revoke** — add the IAM revoke counterpart in `rbgi_IAM.sh` and call it from teardown; keep the DRS-400 tolerance on the grant path (now relocated to establish, ≈once per identity).
- **Refactor both layers coherently** — `rbgg_Governor.sh` (`rbgg_invest_director/retriever`, `zrbgg_divest_role`) and the Rust canonical `rbtdrk_role_invest_impl` (`rbtd/src/rbtdrk_canonical.rs`) share the verb semantics, so they move together.
- **Verify** — credential changes → `service` suite (needs GCP creds; covers access-probe + hallmark-lifecycle).

**Two scope flags before I touch code:**
1. The memo's scope-note 6 (one-time sweep of the *existing* 145+23+23 ghosts) is **not** in the Done criteria — the split stops new ones, doesn't clean old. I read that as out-of-scope for this pace. Confirm?
2. This is the rare pace where minting new persistent names is itself the embedded judgment — I'll propose the `rbw-a*` colophon set for your sign-off rather than picking unilaterally.

**Execution strategy:** Sequential opus — Bash governor + Rust canonical + IAM helper are interdependent (shared verb semantics), low file independence, and the security-guarantee preservation (blunt-kill, least-privilege) wants careful judgment, not parallel fan-out.

The pace is still `[rough]`. I can either **firm the docket first** (reslate with the verb-surface decision baked in) or **proceed directly** and bring you the colophon proposal as the first concrete step. Which would you prefer — and is my read on the two scope flags right?

content.text:
You're right on both, and they're the same error: I read ahead.

**1.** Ghost sweep out of scope — confirmed, dropped.

**2.** No — colophons aren't a prerequisite for repairing pace 1. The repair is a *function-level* lifecycle separation: `rbgg_invest_*` stops doing SA-create + grant + Key-create as one body, `zrbgg_divest_role` gets its missing revoke, and re-key becomes Key-only. That all happens inside the existing functions and their existing tabtarget surface. Whether `establish`/`re-key`/`teardown` ever get *exposed* as separate tabtargets is a packaging question — downstream, deferrable, and not something the Done criteria need. I asserted "mint new colophons" with no basis; that was me front-running a decision that may never need making.

And reading ₢BYAAB was the same reflex — pulling in the federation / one-file-per-person framing that isn't this pace's concern, which is probably what seeded the colophon tangent in the first place. Mount is about the one actionable pace; I overran it.

So the real first step is to read the actual `rbgg_invest_director/retriever` + `zrbgg_divest_role` bodies and confirm where the three lifecycles are fused, then propose the split at the function level. Want me to go look?

zrbgg_roster_role "${RBCC_account_retriever}" "${RBCC_fact_ext_roster_retriever}"
}

rbgg_roster_directors() {
  zrbgg_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Roster Director service accounts (emit per-identity fact files)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  zrbgg_roster_role "${RBCC_account_director}" "${RBCC_fact_ext_roster_director}"
}

rbgg_invest_retriever() {
  zrbgg_sentinel

  local z_identity="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"

  buc_doc_brief "Invest a Retriever service account for an identity"
  buc_doc_param "identity" "Identity (required) — composes ${RBCC_account_retriever}-<identity>"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${z_identity}" || buc_die "Identity required"

  local z_account_name="${RBCC_account_retriever}-${z_identity}"
  local z_account_email="${z_account_name}@${RBGD_SA_EMAIL_FULL}"

  buc_step "Investing Retriever service account: ${z_account_name}"

  zrbgg_create_service_account_with_key                                          \
    "${z_account_name}"                                                        \
    "Recipe Bottle Retriever (${z_identity})"                                  \
    "Read-only access to Google Artifact Registry - identity: ${z_identity}"   \
    "${RBCC_role_retriever}" > /dev/null || buc_die "Failed to create Retriever SA"

  local z_token
  z_token=$(rba_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token"

  buc_step 'Adding Artifact Registry Reader role'
  rbgi_add_project_iam_role                 \
    "${z_token}"                            \
    "Grant Artifact Registry Reader"        \
    "${RBGD_PROJECT_RESOURCE}"              \
    "${RBGC_ROLE_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_READER}"  \
    "serviceAccount:${z_account_email}"     \
    "retriever-reader"

  buc_step 'Adding Container Analysis Occurrences Viewer role'
  rbgi_add_project_iam_role                              \
    "${z_token}"                                         \
    "Grant Container Analysis Occurrences Viewer"        \
    "${RBGD_PROJECT_RESOURCE}"                           \
    "${RBGC_ROLE_CONTAINERANALYSIS_OCCURRENCES_VIEWER}"  \
    "serviceAccount:${z_account_email}"                  \
    "retriever-analysis"

  buc_info "RBRA file written: ${RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE}"
  buc_info ""
  buc_info "Move the RBRA file to a safe place — typically delivered to the"
  buc_info "operator who consumes this identity. When the invester is also the"
  buc_info "consumer, the local role slot is the canonical destination:"
  buc_bare "        mv ${RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE} ${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}"
}

rbgg_invest_director() {
  zrbgg_sentinel

  local z_identity="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"

  buc_doc_brief "Invest a Director service account for an identity"
  buc_doc_param "identity" "Identity (required) — composes ${RBCC_account_director}-<identity>"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${z_identity}" || buc_die "Identity required"

  local z_account_name="${RBCC_account_director}-${z_identity}"
  local z_account_email="${z_account_name}@${RBGD_SA_EMAIL_FULL}"

  buc_step "Investing Director service account: ${z_account_name}"

  zrbgg_create_service_account_with_key                      \
    "${z_account_name}"                                    \
    "Recipe Bottle Director (${z_identity})"               \
    "Create/destroy container images for ${z_identity}"    \
    "${RBCC_role_director}" > /dev/null || buc_die "Failed to create Director SA"

  buc_step 'Get OAuth token from admin'
  local z_token
  z_token=$(rba_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token"

  buc_step 'Adding Cloud Build Editor role (project scope)'
  rbgi_add_project_iam_role                 \
    "${z_token}"                            \
    "Grant Cloud Build Editor"              \
    "${RBGD_PROJECT_RESOURCE}"              \
    "${RBGC_ROLE_CLOUDBUILD_BUILDS_EDITOR}" \
    "serviceAccount:${z_account_email}"     \
    "director-cb"

  rbgi_add_project_iam_role                 \
    "${z_token}"                            \
    "Grant Project Viewer"                  \
    "${RBGD_PROJECT_RESOURCE}"              \
    "roles/viewer"                          \
    "serviceAccount:${z_account_email}"     \
    "director-viewer"

  rbgi_add_project_iam_role                 \
    "${z_token}"                            \
    "Grant Worker Pool User"               \
    "${RBGD_PROJECT_RESOURCE}"              \
    "roles/cloudbuild.workerPoolUser"       \
    "serviceAccount:${z_account_email}"     \
    "director-pool"

  buc_step 'Grant serviceAccountUser on Mason'
  rbgi_add_sa_iam_role "${z_token}" "${RBGD_MASON_EMAIL}" "${z_account_email}" "roles/iam.serviceAccountUser"

  buc_step 'Grant Artifact Registry roles (complete expected policy)'
  # Complete policy: Director repoAdmin + Mason writer in one setIamPolicy.
  # Prevents read-modify-write race where stale getIamPolicy omits Mason's binding.
  local -r z_gar_resource="projects/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/locations/${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}/repositories/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
  local -r z_gar_get_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY}${RBGC_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_V1}/${z_gar_resource}:getIamPolicy?options.requestedPolicyVersion=3"
  local -r z_gar_set_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY}${RBGC_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_V1}/${z_gar_resource}:setIamPolicy"

  # Propagation retry — AR repo is resource-scope: member-visibility 400s plus
  # caller-recently-empowered 403 from the resource-scope IAM cache (the
  # governor's roles/owner grant may not yet have reached the AR cache).
  local -ra z_gar_tolerance=(
    "400" "*does not exist*"
    "400" "*is not deleted*"
    "403" ""
  )
  local z_gar_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC}
  local z_gar_prop_elapsed=0
  local -r z_gar_prop_deadline=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_DEADLINE_SEC}
  local z_gar_prop_attempt=0
  local z_gar_get_infix=""
  local z_gar_set_infix=""
  local z_gar_get_code=""

  while :; do
    z_gar_prop_attempt=$((z_gar_prop_attempt + 1))
    z_gar_get_infix="director_gar_get_iam-${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s"
    z_gar_set_infix="director_gar_set_iam-${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s"

    rbuh_json "GET" "${z_gar_get_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_gar_get_infix}"
    z_gar_get_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${z_gar_get_infix}") || z_gar_get_code=""

    # Propagation retry on GET — covers newly-empowered governor (403) and
    # newly-created Director SA member-visibility lag (400 patterns).
    if zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate "${z_gar_get_infix}" "${z_gar_get_code}" "${z_gar_tolerance[@]}"; then
      test "${z_gar_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_gar_prop_deadline}" \
        || buc_die "GAR IAM: propagation timeout after ${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s"
      buc_log_args "GAR getIamPolicy returned ${z_gar_get_code} (propagation delay; attempt ${z_gar_prop_attempt}, ${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s)"
      sleep "${z_gar_prop_delay}"
      z_gar_prop_elapsed=$((z_gar_prop_elapsed + z_gar_prop_delay))
      z_gar_prop_delay=$((z_gar_prop_delay * 2))
      test "${z_gar_prop_delay}" -le "${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}" || z_gar_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}
      continue
    fi

    rbuh_require_ok "Get GAR repo IAM policy" "${z_gar_get_infix}"

    # Build complete expected policy: Director repoAdmin + Mason writer
    local z_gar_partial
    z_gar_partial=$(rbgi_jq_add_member_to_role_capture "${z_gar_get_infix}" \
      "roles/artifactregistry.repoAdmin" "serviceAccount:${z_account_email}" "") \
      || buc_die "Failed to add Director repoAdmin to GAR IAM policy"

    local z_gar_intermediate="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbuh_director_gar_complete_iam_u_resp.json"
    printf '%s\n' "${z_gar_partial}" > "${z_gar_intermediate}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to write intermediate GAR IAM policy"

    local z_gar_complete
    z_gar_complete=$(rbgi_jq_add_member_to_role_capture "director_gar_complete_iam" \
      "roles/artifactregistry.writer" "serviceAccount:${RBGD_MASON_EMAIL}" "") \
      || buc_die "Failed to add Mason writer to GAR IAM policy"

    local z_gar_set_body="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgg_gar_complete_policy_body.json"
    printf '{"policy":%s}\n' "${z_gar_complete}" > "${z_gar_set_body}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to write GAR setIamPolicy body"
    rbuh_json "POST" "${z_gar_set_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_gar_set_infix}" "${z_gar_set_body}"

    local z_gar_set_code
    z_gar_set_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${z_gar_set_infix}") || buc_die "No HTTP code from GAR setIamPolicy"

    # Propagation retry on SET — same tolerance list as GET.
    if zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate "${z_gar_set_infix}" "${z_gar_set_code}" "${z_gar_tolerance[@]}"; then
      test "${z_gar_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_gar_prop_deadline}" \
        || buc_die "GAR IAM: propagation timeout after ${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s"
      buc_log_args "GAR setIamPolicy returned ${z_gar_set_code} (propagation delay; attempt ${z_gar_prop_attempt}, ${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s)"
      sleep "${z_gar_prop_delay}"
      z_gar_prop_elapsed=$((z_gar_prop_elapsed + z_gar_prop_delay))
      z_gar_prop_delay=$((z_gar_prop_delay * 2))
      test "${z_gar_prop_delay}" -le "${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}" || z_gar_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}
      continue
    fi

    rbuh_require_ok "Set GAR repo IAM policy (complete)" "${z_gar_set_infix}"
    break
  done

  buc_info "RBRA file written: ${RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE}"
  buc_info ""
  buc_info "Move the RBRA file to a safe place — typically delivered to the"
  buc_info "operator who consumes this identity. When the invester is also the"
  buc_info "consumer, the local role slot is the canonical destination:"
  buc_bare "        mv ${RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE} ${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"
}

# Divest a single role+identity: synthesize email, DELETE (404-tolerant),
# opportunistic role-RBRA cleanup only on email match.
zrbgg_divest_role() {
  zrbgg_sentinel

  local -r z_role="$1"
  local -r z_identity="$2"
  local -r z_role_rbra_file="$3"

  local z_account_email="${z_role}-${z_identity}@${RBGD_SA_EMAIL_FULL}"

  buc_step "Divesting service account: ${z_account_email}"

  buc_log_args 'Get OAuth token from admin'
  local z_token
  z_token=$(rba_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token"

  buc_log_args 'Delete via REST API (404-tolerant)'
  rbuh_json "DELETE" "${RBGD_API_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS}/${z_account_email}" "${z_token}" \
                                                 "${ZRBGG_INFIX_DELETE}"
  rbuh_require_ok "Delete service account" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_DELETE}" \
    404 "not found (already deleted)"

  buc_log_args 'Confirm deletion propagated before any same-name recreate'
  # When the DELETE actually removed an SA, wait until the GET path reports it
  # gone — a delete-accepted response precedes propagation, and an immediately
  # following same-name invest would otherwise race a still-visible account.
  # Skipped on 404: the account was already absent, nothing to wait for.
  local z_delete_code
  z_delete_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${ZRBGG_INFIX_DELETE}") || z_delete_code=""
  if test "${z_delete_code}" != "404"; then
    rbuh_poll_until_gone "Service account ${z_account_email}" \
      "${RBGD_API_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS}/${z_account_email}" "${z_token}" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_DELETE_GONE}"
  fi

  buc_log_args 'Opportunistic role-RBRA cleanup if installed credential matches'
  if test -f "${z_role_rbra_file}"; then
    local z_installed_email
    z_installed_email=$(. "${z_role_rbra_file}" 2>/dev/null && printf '%s' "${RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL:-}") \
      || z_installed_email=""
    if test "${z_installed_email}" = "${z_account_email}"; then
      rm -f "${z_role_rbra_file}"
      buc_info "Removed installed RBRA at ${z_role_rbra_file} (matched divested email)"
    fi
  fi

  buc_success "Divest operation completed"
}

rbgg_divest_retriever() {
  zrbgg_sentinel

  local z_identity="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"

  buc_doc_brief "Divest a Retriever service account by identity"
  buc_doc_param "identity" "Identity (required) — composes ${RBCC_account_retriever}-<identity>"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${z_identity}" || buc_die "Identity required"

  zrbgg_divest_role "${RBCC_account_retriever}" "${z_identity}" "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}"
}

rbgg_divest_director() {
  zrbgg_sentinel

  local z_identity="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"

  buc_doc_brief "Divest a Director service account by identity"
  buc_doc_param "identity" "Identity (required) — composes ${RBCC_account_director}-<identity>"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${z_identity}" || buc_die "Identity required"

  zrbgg_divest_role "${RBCC_account_director}" "${z_identity}" "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"
}

rbgg_destroy_project() {
  zrbgg_sentinel
  buc_doc_brief "DEPRECATED: Use rbgp_project_delete instead - moved to Payor module for billing/destructive ops"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_warn "========================================================================"
  buc_warn "DEPRECATION NOTICE: rbgg_destroy_project is deprecated"
  buc_warn "========================================================================"
  buc_warn ""
  buc_warn "Project deletion has been moved to the Payor module which handles"
  buc_warn "all billing and destructive lifecycle operations."
  buc_warn ""
  buc_warn "Use instead:"

159	zrbgg_create_service_account_with_key() {
160	  zrbgg_sentinel
161	
162	  local z_account_name="$1"
163	  local z_display_name="$2"
164	  local z_description="$3"
165	  local z_role="$4"
166	
167	  local z_account_email="${z_account_name}@${RBGD_SA_EMAIL_FULL}"
168	
169	  buc_step 'Get OAuth token from admin'
170	  local z_token
171	  z_token=$(rba_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token"
172	
173	  buc_step "Preflight: confirm ${z_account_name} does not already exist"
174	  # Invest is fail-loud by design (RBSRK/RBSDK): a pre-existing SA is treated as
175	  # state drift, not an idempotent rerun. The operator clears it with the
176	  # matching GovernorDivests verb (rbw-arD/rbw-adD) before re-investing.
177	  rbuh_json "GET" "${RBGD_API_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS}/${z_account_email}" "${z_token}" \
178	                                                 "${ZRBGG_INFIX_PREFLIGHT}"
179	  local z_preflight_code
180	  z_preflight_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${ZRBGG_INFIX_PREFLIGHT}") || z_preflight_code=""
181	  case "${z_preflight_code}" in
182	    404) buc_log_args "Service account ${z_account_email} absent — clear to create" ;;
183	    200) buc_die "Service account ${z_account_email} already exists — run the matching GovernorDivests verb (rbw-arD/rbw-adD) first to re-key" ;;
184	    *)   buc_die "Preflight GET for ${z_account_email} returned unexpected HTTP ${z_preflight_code}" ;;
185	  esac
186	
187	  buc_step "Create request JSON for ${z_account_name}"
188	  jq -n                                      \
189	    --arg account_id "${z_account_name}"     \
190	    --arg display_name "${z_display_name}"   \
191	    --arg description "${z_description}"     \
192	    '{
193	      accountId: $account_id,
194	      serviceAccount: {
195	        displayName: $display_name,
196	        description: $description
197	      }
198	    }' > "${ZRBGG_PREFIX}create_request.json" || buc_die "Failed to create request JSON"
199	
200	  buc_step 'Create service account via REST API'
201	  rbuh_json "POST" "${RBGD_API_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS}" "${z_token}" \
202	    "${ZRBGG_INFIX_CREATE}" "${ZRBGG_PREFIX}create_request.json"
203	  rbuh_require_ok "Create service account" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_CREATE}"
204	
205	  local z_sa_uid
206	  z_sa_uid=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${ZRBGG_INFIX_CREATE}" '.uniqueId') \
207	    || buc_die "Failed to get uniqueId from SA creation response"
208	  buc_info "Service account created: ${z_account_email} (uid: ${z_sa_uid})"
209	
210	  rbuh_poll_until_ok "SA propagation (by email)" \
211	    "${RBGD_API_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS}/${z_account_email}" "${z_token}" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_VERIFY}"
212	
213	  buc_step 'Preflight: ensure no existing USER_MANAGED keys (manual cleanup path)'
214

zrbgg_create_service_account_with_key() {
  zrbgg_sentinel

  local z_account_name="$1"
  local z_display_name="$2"
  local z_description="$3"
  local z_role="$4"

  local z_account_email="${z_account_name}@${RBGD_SA_EMAIL_FULL}"

  buc_step 'Get OAuth token from admin'
  local z_token
  z_token=$(rba_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token"

  buc_step "Preflight: confirm ${z_account_name} does not already exist"
  # Invest is fail-loud by design (RBSRK/RBSDK): a pre-existing SA is treated as
  # state drift, not an idempotent rerun. The operator clears it with the
  # matching GovernorDivests verb (rbw-arD/rbw-adD) before re-investing.
  rbuh_json "GET" "${RBGD_API_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS}/${z_account_email}" "${z_token}" \
                                                 "${ZRBGG_INFIX_PREFLIGHT}"
  local z_preflight_code
  z_preflight_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${ZRBGG_INFIX_PREFLIGHT}") || z_preflight_code=""
  case "${z_preflight_code}" in
    404) buc_log_args "Service account ${z_account_email} absent — clear to create" ;;
    200) buc_die "Service account ${z_account_email} already exists — run the matching GovernorDivests verb (rbw-arD/rbw-adD) first to re-key" ;;
    *)   buc_die "Preflight GET for ${z_account_email} returned unexpected HTTP ${z_preflight_code}" ;;
  esac

  buc_step "Create request JSON for ${z_account_name}"
  jq -n                                      \
    --arg account_id "${z_account_name}"     \
    --arg display_name "${z_display_name}"   \
    --arg description "${z_description}"     \
    '{
      accountId: $account_id,
      serviceAccount: {
        displayName: $display_name,
        description: $description
      }
    }' > "${ZRBGG_PREFIX}create_request.json" || buc_die "Failed to create request JSON"

  buc_step 'Create service account via REST API'
  rbuh_json "POST" "${RBGD_API_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS}" "${z_token}" \
    "${ZRBGG_INFIX_CREATE}" "${ZRBGG_PREFIX}create_request.json"
  rbuh_require_ok "Create service account" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_CREATE}"

  local z_sa_uid
  z_sa_uid=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${ZRBGG_INFIX_CREATE}" '.uniqueId') \
    || buc_die "Failed to get uniqueId from SA creation response"
  buc_info "Service account created: ${z_account_email} (uid: ${z_sa_uid})"

  rbuh_poll_until_ok "SA propagation (by email)" \
    "${RBGD_API_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS}/${z_account_email}" "${z_token}" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_VERIFY}"

  buc_step 'Preflight: ensure no existing USER_MANAGED keys (manual cleanup path)'

  # Subresource read-path can lag SA email-path readiness — flap observed
  # 200→200→404 on freshly-minted depots. Retry on the actual call, not a
  # proxy probe (see RBSCIP "SA keys subresource propagation").
  local z_list_attempt=0
  local z_list_infix=""
  local z_list_code=""
  while :; do
    z_list_attempt=$((z_list_attempt + 1))
    z_list_infix="${ZRBGG_INFIX_LIST_KEYS}-attempt${z_list_attempt}"
    rbuh_json "GET"                                                        \
      "${RBGD_API_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS}/${z_account_email}${RBGC_PATH_KEYS}"        \
                                        "${z_token}" "${z_list_infix}"

    z_list_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${z_list_infix}") || z_list_code=""

    if test "${z_list_code}" = "200"; then
      break
    fi

    if test "${z_list_code}" = "404" && test "${z_list_attempt}" -lt "${RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_MAX}"; then
      buc_warn "keys.list returned 404 (SA read-path propagation delay), retry ${z_list_attempt}/${RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_MAX} in ${RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_DELAY_SEC}s..."
      sleep "${RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_DELAY_SEC}"
      continue
    fi

    rbuh_require_ok "List service account keys" "${z_list_infix}"
  done

  buc_log_args 'Count existing user-managed keys'
  local z_user_keys
  z_user_keys=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${z_list_infix}" \
                 '[.keys[]? | select(.keyType=="USER_MANAGED")] | length') \
    || buc_die "Failed to parse service account keys"

  if test "${z_user_keys}" -gt 0; then
    buc_log_args 'Provide a console URL to delete keys manually, then rerun this command'
    local z_sa_email_enc="${z_account_email//@/%40}"
    local z_keys_url="${RBGC_CONSOLE_URL}iam-admin/serviceaccounts/details/${z_sa_email_enc}?project=${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}"

    buc_warn "Found ${z_user_keys} existing USER_MANAGED key(s) on ${z_account_email}."
    buc_info "Open Console, select the **Keys** tab, delete old keys, then rerun:"
    buc_info "  ${z_keys_url}"
    buc_die  "Aborting to avoid minting additional keys."
  fi

  buc_step 'Generate service account key (with propagation retry)'
  local z_key_req="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgg_key_request.json"
  printf '%s' '{"privateKeyType": "TYPE_GOOGLE_CREDENTIALS_FILE"}' > "${z_key_req}"

  local z_key_attempt=0
  local z_key_infix=""
  local z_key_code=""
  while :; do
    z_key_attempt=$((z_key_attempt + 1))
    z_key_infix="${ZRBGG_INFIX_KEY}-attempt${z_key_attempt}"
    rbuh_json "POST" "${RBGD_API_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS}/${z_account_email}${RBGC_PATH_KEYS}" \
                                           "${z_token}" "${z_key_infix}" "${z_key_req}"

    z_key_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${z_key_infix}") || z_key_code=""

    if test "${z_key_code}" = "200"; then
      break
    fi

    if test "${z_key_code}" = "404" && test "${z_key_attempt}" -lt "${RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_MAX}"; then
      buc_warn "keys.create returned 404 (SA write-path propagation delay), retry ${z_key_attempt}/${RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_MAX} in ${RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_DELAY_SEC}s..."
      sleep "${RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_DELAY_SEC}"
      continue
    fi

    rbuh_require_ok "Generate service account key" "${z_key_infix}"
  done

  buc_step 'Extract and decode key data'
  local z_key_b64
  z_key_b64=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${z_key_infix}" '.privateKeyData') \
    || buc_die "Failed to extract privateKeyData"
  # Decode into the assay subdirectory (RBRR_SECRETS_DIR/assay/) so the
  # only readable form of the private key shares lifecycle and location
  # with the final RBRA file — credentials never leak into BURD_TEMP_DIR.
  local -r z_assay_dir="${RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE%/*}"
  local -r z_key_json="${z_assay_dir}/_decoded_key_${z_account_name}.json"
  rbgo_base64_decode_string_to_file "${z_key_b64}" "${z_key_json}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to decode key data"

  buc_step 'Convert JSON key to RBRA format'
  local z_rbra_file="${RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE}"

  local z_client_email
  z_client_email=$(zrbgg_secrets_json_field_capture "${z_key_json}" '.client_email') \
    || buc_die "Failed to extract client_email from key JSON"

  local z_private_key
  z_private_key=$(zrbgg_secrets_json_field_capture "${z_key_json}" '.private_key') \
    || buc_die "Failed to extract private_key from key JSON"

  local z_project_id
  z_project_id=$(zrbgg_secrets_json_field_capture "${z_key_json}" '.project_id') \
    || buc_die "Failed to extract project_id from key JSON"

  buc_step 'Write RBRA file' "${z_rbra_file}"
  # CAUTION: jq -r unescapes JSON \n to real newlines, so z_private_key holds a
  # multi-line PEM string. printf '%s' below preserves real newlines into the
  # RBRA file. Consumer (rbgo_OAuth.sh:zrbgo_build_jwt_capture) tolerates either

content.text:
I've looked. The fusion is localized and the split is clean at the function level — here's the grounded picture.

## Where the three lifecycles fuse

One helper carries the whole H2 surface: **`zrbgg_create_service_account_with_key`** (`rbgg_Governor.sh:159`). It splits almost exactly at line 212:
- **Lines 173–211** = SA lifecycle: fail-loud preflight + create + propagation-poll.
- **Lines 213–330** = Key lifecycle: key preflight + create + decode + RBRA write.

The two invest verbs (`rbgg_invest_retriever:572`, `rbgg_invest_director:623`) call that helper, then append the **Binding grants** (`rbgi_add_project_iam_role` ×2–3, plus Mason `add_sa_iam_role` and the GAR repo grant for director). `zrbgg_divest_role:772` is SA-delete only.

So the natural split needs **no new colophons** — it's: `zrbgg_create_service_account_with_key` → `zrbgg_ensure_service_account` (the 173–211 half, made idempotent) + `zrbgg_rekey_service_account` (the 213–330 half), with the grant blocks relocated into an establish path and a new revoke path for teardown.

## Three things the look surfaced that the memo's framing didn't fully carry

These are the real decisions, and they're all at the establish/re-key boundary the Character flagged:

1. **Revoke is net-new, not merely "unwired."** `rbgi_IAM.sh` has *only* `add_*` helpers — there is no remove/revoke function anywhere. H1 isn't "a revoke that's never called"; it's "no revoke exists." Teardown must add read-modify-write member-removal mirroring every grant we issue (project-scope, Mason SA-scope, GAR repo-scope). This is the largest new chunk.

2. **Blunt-kill isn't automated today.** The key preflight (`:249–258`) *aborts to a manual console cleanup* when USER_MANAGED keys exist. Today the old key dies only because re-key nukes the whole SA — that fusion *is* H2. So re-key needs a real `keys.delete` loop written from scratch; "blunt-kill preserved" actually means "newly automate what the SA-delete used to do incidentally."

3. **Idempotent establish contradicts a documented spec contract.** The preflight comment (`:174–185`) cites **RBSRK/RBSDK**: a pre-existing SA is "state drift, not an idempotent rerun" — fail-loud is *specified*, not incidental. Making establish idempotent means amending RBSRK/RBSDK (and the divest specs RBSRD/RBSDD to add revoke + teardown). That's a spec change riding alongside the code.

## My read

The code split is mechanical and well-contained, as the Character promised — but two of the three operations (revoke, automated key-delete) are net-new machinery rather than rearrangement, and the idempotency flip touches four specs. That's more than "move lines around," and it sharpens why this pace is still `[rough]`.

I deliberately stopped before reading the Rust canonical (`rbtdrk_role_invest_impl`) — that's the test-orchestration mirror and I didn't want to over-read again. 

Two ways forward: I can **read the Rust side** to size the full blast radius, or **firm the docket now** (reslate ₢BYAAA with the function-level split + the three seams baked in, so the spec-amendment scope is explicit before any code moves). Which do you want?

message.content: read rust next.  We won't reslate if we can finish all work in this chat.

content.text: Let me read the shared invest body in detail.

content.text:
The Rust side is a **consumer** of the verb surface, and it's exactly where H2 bites the tests. Here's what I found plus the consolidated blast radius.

## Rust: the divest-then-invest dance is H2's test-side scar

`rbtdrk_role_invest_impl` (`rbtdrk_canonical.rs:704`) does a **divest → invest** pair on every run. The comment at `:719–723` says why outright:

> *"Invest is fail-loud if the SA already exists (RBSRK/RBSDK preflight), so a standing-depot rerun must clear the prior SA first."*

That per-rerun SA-delete **is** the delete→recreate race that "blocked dogfight." Once invest is idempotent, this whole pre-divest (`:724–741`) is deleted — the test just calls invest. So the Rust change is a *simplification*, and removing it is what stops the tombstone accrual + the race in the gauntlet/skirmish suites. The `divest_colophon` param and the `RBTDGC_DIVEST_*` constants fall out of this path (callers at `:659`, `:681`).

## Consolidated blast radius — no new colophons, no new tabtargets

| File | Change | Net-new? |
|------|--------|----------|
| `rbgg_Governor.sh` | Split `zrbgg_create_service_account_with_key:159` at line 212 → `zrbgg_ensure_service_account` (the `200`→`buc_die` at `:183` becomes an idempotent no-op) + `zrbgg_rekey_service_account` | rearrange |
| `rbgg_Governor.sh` | `zrbgg_divest_role:772` gains a binding-revoke pass before the SA-delete → becomes teardown | + revoke call |
| `rbgg_Governor.sh` | Automated `keys.delete` loop — today `:249–258` *aborts to manual console cleanup*; re-key must actually delete | **net-new** |
| `rbgi_IAM.sh` | `rbgi_remove_*_iam_role` + a member-removal jq helper mirroring `rbgi_jq_add_member_to_role_capture:813`, across project + Mason-SA + GAR-repo scopes | **net-new, largest** |
| `rbtdrk_canonical.rs` | Delete the pre-divest (`:724–741`); drop `divest_colophon` param + update 2 callers | simplify |
| RBSRK / RBSDK / RBSRD / RBSDD | invest fail-loud→idempotent-establish+rekey; divest→teardown-with-revoke | spec amend |

3 code files + 4 specs. Bounded, but the revoke helper + automated key-delete are real machinery, not line-moving.

## The one judgment — the establish/re-key boundary

The memo wants re-key to "never touch Bindings." But operators only ever want *"give me this identity with a fresh key."* So the question: do establish and re-key need separate **operator verbs**, or one idempotent verb?

My recommendation: **one idempotent `invest` verb, three internal functions.** `invest` calls `ensure_service_account` (short-circuits the grants when the SA already carries them — so steady-state re-key stays Key-only, satisfying the memo) then `rekey_service_account` (always rotates). `divest` becomes teardown. This:
- closes H1 (grants idempotent → no accrual; revoke wired on teardown) and H2 (SA never deleted on the hot path),
- keeps the operator surface at the two existing tabtargets,
- preserves the memo's "re-key presumes established, touches only Keys" *as the steady-state behavior* via the short-circuit,
- collapses the Rust dance cleanly (idempotent invest = safe to call on any rerun).

The cost: a cold first-invest still runs the grant (and its one-time DRS-400 exposure — scope-note 5, kept covered by `rbgi`'s tolerance). That's the relocation the memo already accepts.

## Finishability read (you asked)

The **code + spec** work is finishable in this chat. **Verification** is the catch: credential changes want the `service` tier, which hits the live standing depot `cancbhm-d-canest3bhm100001` and needs GCP creds present here — otherwise the final green is a run you drive. So: implement fully now, but calibrate that the last mile may be a live run rather than an in-chat pass.

Two things before I cut code: **(a)** does the one-idempotent-verb boundary land for you, or do you want establish/re-key as genuinely separate operator actions? **(b)** the revoke helper is the net-new heart — want me to start there?

content: Mounted ₣BY pace ₢BYAAA: split the fused credential invest into idempotent establish, re-key, and teardown across the governor, IAM, and Rust test code. I've finished surveying all the code; next I need your call on whether one idempotent invest verb works before I start cutting code. (disable recaps in /config)

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# Recipe Bottle Google IAM - Implementation
20	
21	# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
22	# Operational Invariants (RBGI is single writer; 409 is fatal)
23	#
24	# - Single admin actor: All RBGI operations are executed by a single admin
25	#   identity. There are no concurrent writers in the same project.
26	# - Pristine-state expectation: RBGI init/creation flows assume the project
27	#   is pristine for the resources they manage. If a resource "already exists"
28	#   (HTTP 409), that's treated as state drift or prior manual activity.
29	# - Policy: All HTTP 409 Conflict responses are fatal (buc_die). We do not
30	#   treat 409 as idempotent success anywhere in RBGI.
31	#   If you see a 409, resolve state drift first (destroy/reset), then rerun.
32	# - Note: 409 has two distinct meanings in RBGI operations:
33	#   (a) Resource creation: "already exists" — state drift or prior manual activity.
34	#   (b) setIamPolicy: "ABORTED" — etag mismatch from concurrent policy change.
35	#   Both are fatal under single-writer invariant. Google-internal auto-provisioning
36	#   can trigger (b) outside our control; the 409 surfaces a real concurrency issue.
37	# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
38	
39	set -euo pipefail
40	
41	# Multiple inclusion detection
42	test -z "${ZRBGI_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgi multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
43	ZRBGI_SOURCED=1
44	
45	######################################################################
46	# Internal Functions (zrbgi_*)
47	
48	zrbgi_kindle() {
49	  test -z "${ZRBGI_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgi already kindled"
50	
51	  # Validate dependencies
52	  buv_dir_exists "${BURD_TEMP_DIR}"
53	
54	  # Ensure dependencies are kindled
55	  zrbgc_sentinel
56	  zrbuh_sentinel
57	  zrbge_sentinel
58	
59	  # Module prefix for temp files
60	  readonly ZRBGI_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgi_"
61	  readonly ZRBGI_EMPTY_JSON="${ZRBGI_PREFIX}empty.json"
62	  printf '{}' > "${ZRBGI_EMPTY_JSON}"
63	
64	  readonly ZRBGI_VERSION3_BODY="${ZRBGI_PREFIX}version3_body.json"
65	  printf '%s\n' '{"options":{"requestedPolicyVersion":3}}' > "${ZRBGI_VERSION3_BODY}"
66	
67	  # Infix values for IAM operations
68	  readonly ZRBGI_INFIX_ROLE="role"
69	  readonly ZRBGI_INFIX_ROLE_SET="role_set"
70	  readonly ZRBGI_INFIX_REPO_ROLE="repo_role"
71	  readonly ZRBGI_INFIX_REPO_ROLE_SET="repo_role_set"
72	  readonly ZRBGI_INFIX_SA_IAM_VERIFY="sa_iamverify"
73	  readonly ZRBGI_INFIX_BUCKET_IAM="bucket_iam"
74	  readonly ZRBGI_INFIX_BUCKET_IAM_SET="bucket_iam_set"
75	  readonly ZRBGI_INFIX_SECRET_IAM="secret_iam"
76	  readonly ZRBGI_INFIX_SECRET_IAM_SET="secret_iam_set"
77	
78	  readonly ZRBGI_POSTFIX_JSON="_i_resp.json"
79	
80	  readonly ZRBGI_KINDLED=1
81	}
82	
83	zrbgi_sentinel() {
84	  test "${ZRBGI_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbgi not kindled - call zrbgi_kindle first"
85	}
86	
87	# Classify an HTTP response against a caller-supplied tolerance list.
88	# Each tolerance is a (code, body-glob) pair passed as two positional args
89	# after the infix and code. An empty body-glob matches any response body
90	# for that code — short-circuits without loading the error message.
91	#
92	# Three propagation classes are encoded as tolerance pairs:
93	#   (400, "*does not exist*") — forward member-visibility lag
94	#   (400, "*is not deleted*") — backward member-visibility lag
95	#   (403, "")                 — caller-recently-empowered (resource-scope cache lag)
96	#
97	# Project-scope sites declare only the two 400 patterns; resource-scope sites
98	# (AR repo, SA, bucket, secret) add the 403 pair. Time-bound on 403 is the
99	# discriminator: real propagation succeeds within budget, real denial waits
100	# the budget and fails cleanly.
101	#
102	# Returns 0 (true) if response matches any tolerance pair, 1 otherwise.
103	zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate() {
104	  local -r z_infix="${1}"
105	  local -r z_code="${2}"
106	  shift 2
107	
108	  local z_err_msg=""
109	  local z_err_loaded=0
110	  local z_tol_code=""
111	  local z_tol_glob=""
112	
113	  while test "$#" -ge 2; do
114	    z_tol_code="${1}"
115	    z_tol_glob="${2}"
116	    shift 2
117	
118	    test "${z_code}" = "${z_tol_code}" || continue
119	
120	    test -n "${z_tol_glob}" || return 0
121	
122	    if test "${z_err_loaded}" = "0"; then
123	      z_err_msg=$(rbge_error_message_capture "${z_infix}") || z_err_msg=""
124	      z_err_loaded=1
125	    fi
126	
127	    case "${z_err_msg}" in
128	      ${z_tol_glob}) return 0 ;;
129	    esac
130	  done
131	
132	  return 1
133	}
134	
135	######################################################################
136	# External Functions (rbgi_*)
137	
138	# Add a project-scoped IAM role binding with optimistic concurrency and strong read-back.
139	rbgi_add_project_iam_role() {
140	  zrbgi_sentinel
141	
142	  local -r z_token="${1:-}"
143	  local -r z_label="${2:-}"
144	  local -r z_resource="${3:-}"  # resource_base: Base resource URL
145	  local -r z_role="${4:-}"
146	  local -r z_member="${5:-}"
147	  local -r z_parent_infix="${6:-}"
148	
149	  test -n "${z_token}" || buc_die "Token required"
150	  buc_log_args "Using admin token (value not logged)"
151	
152	  local -r z_resource_path="${z_resource#/}"  # strip leading slash if present
153	  local -r z_base="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/${z_resource_path}"
154	  local -r z_get_url="${z_base}${RBGC_CRM_GET_IAM_POLICY_SUFFIX}"
155	  local -r z_set_url="${z_base}${RBGC_CRM_SET_IAM_POLICY_SUFFIX}"
156	
157	  test -n "${z_resource}" || buc_die "resource required"
158	  test -n "${z_role}"     || buc_die "role required"
159	  test -n "${z_member}"   || buc_die "member required"
160	
161	  buc_log_args "${z_label}: add ${z_member} to ${z_role}"
162	
163	  # Propagation retry — project-scope tolerance: forward/backward
164	  # member-visibility (400 patterns). Class C (403 resource-scope cache lag)
165	  # does not apply at project scope.
166	  local -ra z_tolerance=(
167	    "400" "*does not exist*"
168	    "400" "*is not deleted*"
169	  )
170	  local z_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC}
171	  local z_prop_elapsed=0
172	  local -r z_prop_deadline=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_DEADLINE_SEC}
173	  local z_prop_attempt=0
174	  local z_prop_succeeded=0
175	
176	  while :; do
177	    z_prop_attempt=$((z_prop_attempt + 1))
178	
179	    buc_log_args "1) GET policy (v3) [attempt ${z_prop_attempt}]"
180	    buc_log_args "GET_POLICY_URL_DEBUG z_resource:${z_resource} z_get_url:${z_get_url}"
181	    local z_get_body="${ZRBGI_PREFIX}${z_parent_infix}_get_body.json"
182	    local z_get_infix="${z_parent_infix}-get-${z_prop_elapsed}s"
183	    printf '%s\n' '{"options":{"requestedPolicyVersion":3}}' > "${z_get_body}"
184	    rbuh_json "POST" "${z_get_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_get_infix}" "${z_get_body}"
185	
186	    local z_get_code=""
187	    z_get_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${z_get_infix}") || buc_die "No HTTP code from getIamPolicy"
188	
189	    # Check for propagation error on GET
190	    if zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate "${z_get_infix}" "${z_get_code}" "${z_tolerance[@]}"; then
191	      buc_log_args "${z_label}: getIamPolicy returned ${z_get_code} (propagation delay)"
192	      test "${z_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_prop_deadline}" \
193	        || buc_die "${z_label}: propagation timeout after ${z_prop_elapsed}s waiting for member visibility"
194	      buc_log_args "Retry ${z_prop_attempt} at ${z_prop_elapsed}s (next delay ${z_prop_delay}s)"
195	      sleep "${z_prop_delay}"
196	      z_prop_elapsed=$((z_prop_elapsed + z_prop_delay))
197	      z_prop_delay=$((z_prop_delay * 2))
198	      test "${z_prop_delay}" -le "${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}" || z_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}
199	      continue
200	    fi
201	
202	    # Not a propagation error on GET — require success
203	    rbuh_require_ok "${z_label} (get policy)" "${z_get_infix}"
204	
205	    buc_log_args 'Extract etag; require non-empty'
206	    local z_etag=""
207	    z_etag=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${z_get_infix}" ".etag") || buc_die "Missing etag"
208	    test -n "${z_etag}" || buc_die "Empty etag"
209	
210	    buc_log_args "Using etag ${z_etag}"
211	
212	    buc_log_args '2) Build new policy JSON in temp (bindings unique; version=3; keep etag)'
213	    local z_new_policy_json=""
214	    z_new_policy_json=$(rbgi_jq_add_member_to_role_capture "${z_get_infix}" "${z_role}" "${z_member}" "${z_etag}") \
215	      || buc_die "Failed to compose policy JSON"
216	
217	    local z_set_body="${ZRBGI_PREFIX}${z_parent_infix}_set_body.json"
218	    printf '{"policy":%s}\n' "${z_new_policy_json}" > "${z_set_body}"
219	
220	    buc_log_args '3) setIamPolicy (fatal on 409 — etag mismatch)'
221	    local z_set_elapsed=0
222	    local z_set_infix=""
223	    local z_set_succeeded=0
224	    while :; do
225	      z_set_infix="${z_parent_infix}-set-${z_prop_elapsed}s-${z_set_elapsed}s"
226	      rbuh_json "POST" "${z_set_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_set_infix}" "${z_set_body}"
227	
228	      local z_code=""
229	      z_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${z_set_infix}") || buc_die "No HTTP code"
230	
231	      # Check for propagation error on SET — break inner loop to retry outer
232	      if zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate "${z_set_infix}" "${z_code}" "${z_tolerance[@]}"; then
233	        buc_log_args "${z_label}: setIamPolicy returned ${z_code} (propagation delay)"
234	        break
235	      fi
236	
237	      case "${z_code}" in
238	        200)                 z_set_succeeded=1; break ;;
239	        409)                 buc_die "${z_label}: HTTP 409 ABORTED (etag mismatch — concurrent policy change)" ;;
240	        429|500|502|503|504) buc_log_args "Transient ${z_code} at ${z_set_elapsed}s; retry" ;;
241	        *)                   rbuh_require_ok "${z_label} (set policy)" "${z_set_infix}" "" ;;
242	      esac
243	
244	      test "${z_set_elapsed}" -lt "${RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC}" || buc_die "${z_label}: timeout setting policy"
245	      sleep "${RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC}"
246	      z_set_elapsed=$((z_set_elapsed + RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC))
247	    done
248	
249	    # If setIamPolicy succeeded, break outer propagation loop
250	    test "${z_set_succeeded}" != "1" || { z_prop_succeeded=1; break; }
251	
252	    # setIamPolicy hit propagation error — retry outer loop with fresh getIamPolicy
253	    test "${z_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_prop_deadline}" \
254	      || buc_die "${z_label}: propagation timeout after ${z_prop_elapsed}s waiting for member visibility"
255	    buc_log_args "Retry ${z_prop_attempt} at ${z_prop_elapsed}s (next delay ${z_prop_delay}s)"
256	    sleep "${z_prop_delay}"
257	    z_prop_elapsed=$((z_prop_elapsed + z_prop_delay))
258	    z_prop_delay=$((z_prop_delay * 2))
259	    test "${z_prop_delay}" -le "${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}" || z_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}
260	  done
261	
262	  test "${z_prop_succeeded}" = "1" || buc_die "${z_label}: propagation retry loop exited without success"
263	
264	  # The setIamPolicy 200 is the authoritative grant confirmation: the policy was
265	  # written atomically with this binding. A post-set read-back verify loop was
266	  # removed here. Against a standing depot, repeated divest/invest cycles leave
267	  # same-email `deleted:serviceAccount:...?uid=` tombstones in the policy (accepted
268	  # — re-levy is quota-limited), and the email→uid read reconciliation can lag the
269	  # getIamPolicy read past any bounded poll, producing a false timeout on a grant
270	  # that already succeeded. The read-back never made the grant effective — that
271	  # propagates on GCP's clock regardless and cannot be accelerated by polling — so
272	  # it was non-load-bearing and is gone.
273	  buc_log_args "${z_label}: granted (setIamPolicy 200)"
274	  return 0
275	}
